title: Plant and machine operatives n.e.c.
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Design, develop, and evaluate the operation of marine vessels, ship machinery, and related equipment, such as power supply and propulsion systems. (Based on 151 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General

In an Open Vehicle or Operating Equipment

Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Build wheels by cutting and threading new spokes; align wheels and install, repair, and replace bicycle parts such as brakes and tires.
Disassemble axles to repair, adjust, and replace defective parts using hand tools, and clean and lubricate bicycle parts.
Help customers select bicycles based on size and intended use, and sell bicycles and accessories.
Shape replacement parts and assemble new bicycles using hand tools according to specifications.
Install and adjust speed and gear mechanisms, and assist with equipment or accessory installation.
Operate or tend machines that join or finish shoe parts, and perform routine equipment maintenance such as cleaning and lubricating machines.
Inspect and measure workpieces to ensure conformance to specifications, and examine finished products for quality.
Perform business management duties, such as maintaining records or files and preparing reports.
Use a variety of tools or equipment, and operate or manage safety equipment and use safe work habits.
Monitor the production line, inspecting for problems and ensuring proper operation of machines throughout the process.
Remove finished packaged items from the machine and separate rejected items.
Tend or operate machines that package products and adjust machine components and pressure according to product specifications.
Count and record finished packaged items and supply materials to machines such as spindles and conveyors.
Mix or blend ingredients according to recipes using equipment like agitators and paddles or by controlling vats.
Turn valve controls and press switches to start and adjust equipment for cooking, mixing, or blending.
Observe equipment to detect malfunctions, report issues, and maintain product quality.
Prepare and clean processing areas, including sterilizing vats and cleaning packaging containers.
Select and measure ingredients using appropriate measurement tools, ensuring compliance with specified recipes.
Assemble, align, and clamp workpieces into fixtures for joining, bonding, or welding processes.
Conduct trial runs and perform repairs or adjustments to maintain efficient operation of machinery.
Inspect and test completed products to ensure they conform to specifications using measuring devices.
Record operational information and maintain logs of production, maintenance, and safety activities.
Monitor and calibrate machinery, adjusting controls and valves to maintain optimal operation and safety standards.
Analyze engineering drawings, blueprints, and specifications to plan layouts and operations.
Disassemble and clean equipment for inspection and maintenance to ensure proper functionality.
Stop machines when specified amounts of product have been produced.
Perform routine maintenance such as changing oil, checking batteries, and lubricating equipment and machinery.
Install and connect communications equipment such as amplifiers or repeaters to maintain transmission strength.
Lay underground cable directly in trenches or string it through conduits running through trenches.
Clean or maintain tools and test equipment, and clean work areas using hand tools or chemicals.
Attach workpieces to grinding machines and use welding or brazing equipment to form specified sections or repair cracks.
Inspect workpieces to ensure that surfaces and dimensions meet specifications, and conduct quality checks for defects or variations.
Monitor machine operations to determine whether adjustments are necessary, and stop machines when problems occur.
String cables between structures and pull lines to proper tension during installation.
Dig trenches for underground wires or cables, and remove blockages in bins or other equipment when necessary.
Weigh and mix ingredients for textile processing, and add dyes or chemicals to maintain specified solution properties.
Install, connect, test, or adjust equipment according to specifications, and advise on equipment malfunctions that need attention.
Use hand tools and power tools, such as screwdrivers, wrenches, and pneumatic equipment, for various repair and maintenance tasks.
Estimate costs to repair machinery or equipment and prepare work plans based on damage reports.
Remove and replace broken or worn machine parts using hand and power tools, including grinding and cutting tools.
Fit and weld replacement parts into place and grind down welds to smooth repaired surfaces.
Install and repair safety glass and related materials on vehicles, ensuring proper waterproofing and alignment.
Remove and replace damaged components, such as wheels or window mechanisms, using appropriate safety equipment and tools.
Connect test panels to engines and measure outputs to determine performance indicators, diagnosing issues.
Remove broken glass from vehicles and prepare areas for new installations while ensuring surfaces are clean and dry.
Measure distances from landmarks to identify installation sites for equipment.
Locate punctures in tubeless tires by visual inspection or by immersing inflated tires in water baths.
Replace valve stems, remove puncturing objects, and patch tubes with adhesive rubber patches.
Inspect and repair or adjust propellers or propeller shafts, and document inspection and test results.
Adjust carburetor mixtures, electrical point settings, or timing while motors are running in test tanks.
Start motors, monitor performance for signs of malfunction, and idle motors to determine cooling system effectiveness.
Set starter locks, align and repair steering or throttle controls using gauges and hand tools.
Replace parts such as gears, magneto points, piston rings, or spark plugs, and reassemble engines.
Drive trucks to industrial sites to provide services or respond to emergency calls.
Perform regular maintenance services, such as oil changes, lubrications, and tune-ups on vehicles.
Inspect vehicles for damage and record findings, and repair or replace faulty equipment as needed.
Calibrate and test equipment, locate circuit faults using testing instruments and hand tools.
Confer with customers to determine the nature of problems, explain repairs, and demonstrate equipment use.
Maintain processing, production, and inspection information and reports, and keep records of work orders.
Test drive vehicles and components, using diagnostic devices to ensure proper functioning.
Repair brake systems, diagnose engine management systems, and align wheels and axles of automobiles.
Clean and maintain tools, test equipment, and work areas to ensure proper functioning and safety.
Fabricate or repair structures or components using appropriate tools, following technical manuals or drawings.
Install, adjust, or repair hydraulic or electromagnetic automatic lift mechanisms in vehicles.
Repair and service heating, cooling, and electrical systems in vehicles, ensuring optimal performance.
Inspect, repair, and maintain automotive and mechanical equipment and machinery, such as pumps and compressors.
Disassemble and overhaul internal combustion engines, pumps, generators, transmissions, clutches, and differential units.
Rebuild gas or diesel engines and perform overhauls or replacements of carburetors, blowers, generators, distributors, starters, and pumps.
Diagnose mechanical problems, inspect components for wear, and determine corrections by checking blueprints or repair manuals.
Assemble, install, or repair wiring, electrical or electronic components, pipe systems, plumbing, machinery, or equipment.
Inspect, operate, or test machinery or equipment to diagnose malfunctions and verify efficient operation.
Set up and operate machine tools to repair or fabricate machine parts, jigs, fixtures, or tools.
Adjust, connect, or disconnect wiring, piping, tubing, and other parts to modify equipment performance as needed.
Clean or lubricate machines, tools, work areas, and other objects, using hand tools, power tools, and cleaning equipment.
Disassemble broken or defective equipment to facilitate repair and reassemble after completion, transferring tools and supplies as needed.
Operate machinery and heavy equipment, such as forklifts, and start machines to verify setups and make necessary adjustments.
Inspect installation sites and study work orders, building plans, and installation manuals to determine materials requirements and installation procedures.
Examine systems to locate problems, such as loose connections or broken insulation, and record maintenance information, including test results and repairs made.
Test equipment for overheating, using speed gauges and thermometers, and monitor operation of cutting or slicing machines to detect malfunctions.

Record operational data, such as temperatures, pressures, ingredients used, processing times, or test results, and maintain production records of materials produced.

Report hazardous field situations, and investigate instances of illegal tapping into service lines.
Measure, weigh, and mix chemical ingredients according to specifications, and add treating agents to products.
Clean internal compartments and moving parts, and lubricate wearing surfaces of mechanical parts using oils or other lubricants.
Test and repair circuits and sensors, following wiring and system specifications, to ensure proper operation of electrical and mechanical devices.
Provide customers with cost estimates for equipment installation and recommend whether repair or replacement is a better choice.
Compile and prepare documents, such as invoices or warranties, and record operational records on specified forms.

Adjust or repair electrical or mechanical equipment according to specifications and blueprints.

Install electrical wires and controls, and connect to panels and motors following wiring diagrams.
Operate or adjust equipment to collect geological data and record readings used in prospecting for resources.
Inspect job sites and equipment for safety compliance, checking for proper installation of door mechanisms and structures.
Fabricate and install mechanical parts, ensuring compliance with blueprints and structural requirements.
Lubricate moving parts and perform routine maintenance on machinery and appliances.
